title: feat(text): reject empty upstream completions with 502
author: fisventurous

gist.headline: No more silent treatment
gist.blurb: We taught the API to stop accepting empty boxes from upstream providers. If a model tries to hand us zero tokens, we now throw a proper 502 error instead of passing along the void.
gist.summary: Implemented a provider-agnostic guard in the text API that intercepts empty completions and returns a 502 Bad Gateway error.
gist.impact: Developers will now receive clear error codes instead of confusing empty 200 OK responses when upstream models fail to generate text.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as an inspector, catching an empty box or empty plate before it reaches the user. A '502' sign or stamp represents the new error handling.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny inspector's hat, stamping a glowing '502' on an empty digital box. The round tan nomnom creature watches happily nearby, waiting for real data. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
